b: ^Man, this car fucking sucks. I should totally get a Chinese EV...
^Heat waves emanate from the hood of my car like a hot summer day in Helsingborg.

c-0: ^The hood of the car slides open.
c-1: ^I don't open the hood. For whatever reason. I can always come back when I feel like taking this seriously.

b: ^The coolant reservoir is empty. I should check around the back of the car to see if I can identify the problem.

EngineState: ^The engine looks like a jumbled mess of wires and metal. Some people know what this all means and how to fix it. I'll just have to poke around and hope for the best...

c-0: ^I check the spolarvätska fluid. It's half full, but that shouldn't be an issue.
c-1: ^I pull the dipstick out, methodically wipe it on my coat sleeve, and dip it once more. A bit low, but not enough to kill the engine.
c-2: ^I lean over the car and look at an empty reservoir. It is dry as dust. It's usually a lot more blue.

b: ^Maybe it has to do with the piss puddle I saw behind the car. I should go back and took a look at it.
^That's not good, that means it had to leak out somehow. I should go check near the back of the car for a leak.
^I need to find something to plug the hole, something to bind it tight, and some fluid to replace what I lost. Time to lock in...
^Once I've found all the items, I should go to the clueboard in my trunk and take it from there.

InvestigateHole: ^Now that I've seen the puddle to confirm the coolant leak, I know exactly where to look.

c-0: ^Running my hand along the coolant hose, my thumb catches on a massive puncture.
^I don't have any spare coolant or real tools with me. However, only city detectives need gimmicks, country detectives make do.
^I should search inside the car and around the roadside. I need to find something to plug the hole, something to wrap it with, and some liquid to replace the coolant.
^Once I've found all the items, I should go to the clueboard in my trunk and take it from there.

tutorial: ^That should be enough information to build a profile. Whenever I piece together a major lead or obtain key evidence, a new Suspect may unlock on my Clueboard.
^My first suspect? This busted engine. Well, in a sense at least. I just need to find three items to patch the leak, head over to the trunk, and then connect the suspect to those items.
^It would make more sense if I had a real suspect to work with instead of this busted engine, but I don't really work many actual cases these days...

s0: ^Like my grandma used to say; it's all shits and giggles 'til someone giggles and shits. Let's see if this works...
s1: ^ I am the best god damn detective on Earth. That's why God chose me, and he gave me divine intellect. This better work...
s2: ^ Please work please work please work please work please work please work please work...

b: ^Well, I'll be damned. It actually works.
^Gum in the hole, evidence tape wrapped tight, and the reservoir topped off with stale water.
^I'm so smart and cool. Time to hit the road.

^I'm such a fraud... This isn't working at all! ^I need to go back to my clueboard and rethink those connections. ^Lucky for me, this is just a busted engine. I can rethink this mess and try again. At a real crime scene, I'd only get one shot at the clueboard. ^I should also rethink whether the evidence I submitted is missing something. Just because there's 3 slots on the board doesn't mean that there's only 3 pieces of evidence to find.

^This is getting embarrassing... This is not reflective for a detective of my stature. Perhaps there's something I'm missing...? ^Have I really found everything that I need to solve this? Or is there something missing somehow? If need be, I can always take another look around the scene.
^C'mon. We don't know how we can make it any more obvious what you have to do at this point. Perhaps you should consider a career in game journalism?
^Since you have somewhere you need to get to, we'll just tell you outright: Stop trying to make the Motor Oil work. It's not going to work.
^C'mon, one more try detective. You got this. We believe in you.
^You're actually just fucking with us at this point, right? Because it's funny or something?

^News flash, pal. Nothing about this game is supposed to be funny. If you construe even a single thing in this game as being funny, then we've failed at what we set out to do. Detective games aren't supposed to be funny.
^There, the engine is fixed now! You're the greatest detective in the world, probably. Time to get going!
